title: Auto-generate page slug from title when left blank
description: Creating a page currently requires the user to type a slug: `pages.create` takes `slug: slugSchema` (required) and the admin `CreatePageForm` has a separate slug input. When the slug field is left empty, the system should derive it automatically from the page title (e.g. title 'Get started' â†’ slug 'get-started').
acceptanceCriteria: When the slug is omitted/blank on page creation, a valid slug is derived from the title: lowercased, spaces/punctuation collapsed to single hyphens, diacritics stripped (e.g. 'BotĂŁo' â†’ 'botao', 'Get started' â†’ 'get-started'), trimmed of leading/trailing hyphens â€” always matching the existing `slugSchema` regex `^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$`

If the derived slug collides with an existing page in the same section, it is de-duplicated automatically (e.g. `get-started-2`) rather than throwing a CONFLICT â€” auto-derivation should not fail the user; an explicitly typed slug keeps the existing CONFLICT behavior
If a slug IS typed, it is used as-is and validated exactly as today (no silent rewriting of user input)
A title that yields an empty slug after normalization (e.g. all-emoji/all-symbol title) is handled with a clear validation error, not a broken empty slug
The admin `CreatePageForm` slug field is marked optional with a hint that it will be generated from the title

steps.details: Add a pure `slugify(titulo: string): string` in the server (near `slugSchema` in `apps/server/src/trpc/routers/pages.ts` or a small util): Unicode NFD normalize + strip combining marks for diacritics, lowercase, replace non-alphanumeric runs with a single hyphen, trim leading/trailing hyphens. Cover it with unit tests including accented and symbol-heavy inputs.

steps.description: Make slug optional in the API and derive server-side
steps.details: Change `pages.create` input so `slug` is optional. When absent/blank, derive via `slugify(titulo)`; if the result is empty, throw a BAD_REQUEST explaining the title has no sluggable characters. Keep server-side derivation as the single source of truth.

steps.description: Auto de-duplicate derived slugs within the section
steps.details: For derived slugs only, resolve collisions against `(section_id, slug)` by appending `-2`, `-3`, â€¦ until free (query existing slugs in the section, or catch the UNIQUE violation and retry with the next suffix). Explicitly typed slugs keep raising CONFLICT (`isUniqueViolation` â†’ `slugConflict()`), unchanged.

In `apps/admin/src/features/navigation/SidebarTree.tsx` `CreatePageForm`: make the slug input optional (remove any required constraint), update the placeholder/hint to indicate it is generated from the title when left blank, and stop sending an empty string as a real slug (send undefined so the server derives). Optionally show a live preview of the derived slug.

Unit-test `slugify` and the `pages.create` derivation + de-dup path (blank slug â†’ derived; duplicate title in same section â†’ `-2`; typed duplicate slug â†’ CONFLICT; symbol-only title â†’ BAD_REQUEST). Verify in the browser: create 'Get started' with no slug â†’ `get-started`; create a second 'Get started' â†’ `get-started-2`.

technicalNotes: `slugSchema` (`^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$`) is shared by `create` and `updateSlug` in `pages.ts` â€” the derived slug must satisfy it. Diacritic stripping matters because domain titles are in Portuguese (accents are common).
Deriving on the server (not the client) keeps one canonical implementation and avoids client/server drift; a client-side live preview is fine as long as the server value is authoritative.
